title: Xtra Flaky Portuguese Pastries Flatbread
description: A traditional Portuguese flatbread featuring orange blossom water and beurre-monte, perfect for a midnight craving. This satvik-friendly dish is both fatty and flaky.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, and salt.
Add cubed butter to the dry ingredients and use a pastry blender to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
In a separate bowl, whisk together orange blossom water, beurre-monte, and yeast.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Roll out the dough into a large rectangle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Fold the dough in half lengthwise, like a letter, and then fold it in half again, rotating the dough 90 degrees after each fold.
Roll out the dough to its original size and cut it into 8 equal pieces.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and cook the flatbreads for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
